what_it_is: EDR tools continuously monitor endpoints (laptops, servers, workstations) to detect, investigate, and respond to threats in real time. They record endpoint activity, alert on suspicious behavior, and allow security teams to isolate compromised devices and investigate the attack chain.
problems_it_solves: Ransomware and malware on endpoints
Lateral movement after initial compromise
Slow manual incident response
Blind spots on unmanaged devices

what_it_is: XDR extends EDR by correlating telemetry across endpoints, networks, cloud workloads, and identity systems into a unified detection and response platform. It reduces alert fatigue by linking related events into a single incident view across all layers of the environment.
problems_it_solves: Siloed detection tools producing disconnected alerts
Slow cross-domain investigations
Attacker movement across endpoint, network, and cloud
Analyst overload from high alert volumes

what_it_is: SIEM platforms aggregate and correlate log and event data from across the enterprise â€” firewalls, endpoints, applications, cloud services â€” to detect threats, support compliance reporting, and enable forensic investigation. They are the central hub for security visibility in most SOC environments.
problems_it_solves: Lack of centralized security visibility
Compliance reporting requirements (PCI, HIPAA, SOX)
Detecting multi-stage attacks from disparate log sources
Long attacker dwell time due to missed correlation

what_it_is: SOAR platforms automate repetitive security operations tasks â€” phishing triage, alert enrichment, ticket creation, containment actions â€” by orchestrating workflows across tools. They reduce mean time to respond (MTTR) and free analysts from manual, low-value work.
problems_it_solves: High analyst workload from repetitive alert triage
Slow manual incident response workflows
Tool fragmentation requiring manual hand-offs
Inconsistent response procedures across the team

what_it_is: NDR tools analyze raw network traffic using behavioral analytics and machine learning to detect threats that bypass signature-based controls. By inspecting east-west traffic and encrypted flows, NDR provides visibility into attacker movement that endpoint tools cannot see.
problems_it_solves: Insider threats and lateral movement over the network
Encrypted traffic hiding malware communication
IoT and OT devices that cannot run endpoint agents
Advanced persistent threats (APT) dwelling undetected

what_it_is: TIP solutions aggregate, normalize, and operationalize threat intelligence from commercial feeds, open-source sources, ISACs, and internal telemetry. They help security teams prioritize threats, enrich alerts with context, and share intelligence across tools and partners.
problems_it_solves: Overload of raw threat feeds without context
Slow alert enrichment requiring manual research
No structured process for sharing intelligence with peers
Reactive security posture without adversary insight

what_it_is: CNAPP combines CSPM (cloud misconfiguration), CWPP (workload protection), CIEM (identity entitlements), and container/application security into a single platform. It provides a unified risk view across the full cloud application lifecycle from build time to runtime.
problems_it_solves: Cloud misconfigurations exposing sensitive data
Over-privileged cloud identities
Container and Kubernetes security gaps
Fragmented multi-tool cloud security coverage

what_it_is: CSPM tools continuously assess cloud infrastructure configurations â€” S3 bucket policies, IAM rules, network security groups â€” against security benchmarks and compliance frameworks. They surface misconfigurations before attackers can exploit them.
problems_it_solves: Publicly exposed storage buckets and databases
Drift from security baselines in cloud accounts
Compliance failures in AWS, Azure, and GCP environments
Lack of visibility into multi-cloud risk posture

what_it_is: CWPP solutions protect cloud workloads â€” virtual machines, containers, serverless functions â€” from runtime threats. They provide vulnerability management, behavioral monitoring, and threat detection directly within cloud compute environments.
problems_it_solves: Runtime threats targeting containers and VMs
Vulnerability management across ephemeral cloud workloads
Lack of visibility into serverless function behavior
Workloads that cannot use traditional endpoint agents

what_it_is: DSPM tools discover and classify sensitive data wherever it lives â€” across cloud storage, databases, SaaS apps, and data pipelines â€” and continuously assess the security and access controls protecting it. They help organizations answer: where is sensitive data, who can reach it, and is it properly secured?
problems_it_solves: Unknown locations of sensitive and regulated data
Excessive access to sensitive data stores
Shadow data in cloud environments
Compliance gaps for GDPR, HIPAA, and PCI DSS

what_it_is: DTDR solutions focus on detecting and responding to threats specifically targeting sensitive data â€” bulk downloads, exfiltration attempts, unusual access patterns. They combine data activity monitoring with behavioral analytics to protect against insider threats and external attackers who have reached data stores.
problems_it_solves: Data exfiltration by insiders or compromised accounts
Unusual or unauthorized access to sensitive databases
Slow detection of data-focused attacks after breach
Compliance requirements to monitor data access activity

what_it_is: DLP solutions enforce policies that prevent sensitive data from leaving the organization â€” blocking uploads to unauthorized cloud services, restricting email attachments, and monitoring endpoint file transfers. They combine content inspection with contextual policies to protect regulated data in motion and at rest.
problems_it_solves: Accidental or intentional data leakage via email, USB, or web
Regulatory compliance for PCI, HIPAA, GDPR data handling
Insider threat data exfiltration
Shadow IT uploads to unsanctioned cloud services

what_it_is: DAM solutions monitor and log all activity against databases in real time â€” queries, logins, schema changes, privilege escalations â€” independent of the database itself. They detect anomalies, enforce policies, and provide an audit trail for compliance without impacting database performance.
problems_it_solves: Privileged database account abuse
SQL injection and unauthorized data access
Compliance audit requirements (SOX, PCI, HIPAA)
Lack of visibility into who is querying sensitive databases

what_it_is: A WAF sits in front of web applications and inspects HTTP/HTTPS traffic to block common web attacks â€” SQL injection, cross-site scripting, CSRF, and OWASP Top 10 threats â€” before they reach the application. Modern WAFs also include bot management and API protection capabilities.
problems_it_solves: OWASP Top 10 web application attacks
Bot-driven credential stuffing and scraping
Zero-day application vulnerabilities before patching
DDoS attacks targeting web layers

what_it_is: API security solutions discover all APIs (including shadow and zombie APIs), assess their risk, and protect them at runtime from abuse, data exposure, and injection attacks. As applications increasingly communicate over APIs, traditional WAFs lack the context to protect API-specific traffic patterns.
problems_it_solves: Shadow APIs exposing data without visibility
Broken authentication on API endpoints
Data scraping through legitimate API access
Business logic abuse specific to API workflows

what_it_is: Microsegmentation applies granular, workload-level network policies that limit which systems can communicate with which â€” both in data centers and cloud environments. It implements zero-trust principles at the network layer, containing lateral movement even when an attacker is already inside the network.
problems_it_solves: Lateral movement after initial network compromise
Overly permissive east-west traffic in flat networks
Compliance requirements for network isolation (PCI, HIPAA)
Lack of visibility into internal application communication flows

what_it_is: Identity security is a broad category covering the protection of human and machine identities across enterprise environments. It encompasses identity governance, privileged access management, multi-factor authentication, and identity threat detection â€” all built on the premise that identity is the new perimeter.
problems_it_solves: Credential theft enabling account takeover
Over-privileged accounts and excessive access rights
Lack of visibility into identity-based attacks
Complex hybrid identity environments (on-prem + cloud)

what_it_is: IGA platforms manage the full lifecycle of digital identities and their access rights â€” provisioning accounts when users join, updating them as roles change, and revoking access when they leave (joiner-mover-leaver). They automate access certification campaigns, enforce segregation of duties, and provide auditable evidence that users only have the access they are authorized for.
problems_it_solves: Orphaned accounts and access lingering after employees leave
Access creep where users accumulate excessive rights over time
Manual access review processes that are slow and error-prone
Segregation of duties violations creating compliance risk
Audit failures due to inability to prove who had access to what

what_it_is: CIAM solutions manage the identities and authentication journeys of external customers â€” providing registration, login, MFA, consent management, and profile management at consumer scale. Unlike enterprise IAM, CIAM prioritizes user experience and handles millions of identities with high availability requirements.
problems_it_solves: Fragmented customer identity silos across products
Account takeover targeting customer credentials
Compliance with GDPR consent and data subject rights
Poor login experience driving customer abandonment

what_it_is: PAM solutions protect, control, and audit access to privileged accounts â€” domain admins, root accounts, service accounts, and shared credentials â€” that give attackers the highest-value targets in the enterprise. They enforce just-in-time access, session recording, and password vaulting to limit the blast radius of compromised credentials.
problems_it_solves: Compromised admin credentials enabling full network takeover
Shared privileged accounts with no accountability trail
Permanent standing access to production systems
Compliance requirements to audit privileged session activity

what_it_is: MFA requires users to prove identity using at least two factors â€” something they know (password), something they have (authenticator app or hardware token), or something they are (biometrics). It is the single most effective control against credential-based attacks, blocking the vast majority of account takeover attempts.
problems_it_solves: Password theft and credential stuffing attacks
Phishing attacks harvesting credentials
Compliance mandates requiring strong authentication
Weak passwords as the primary authentication control

what_it_is: Passwordless authentication eliminates the password entirely, replacing it with phishing-resistant factors such as FIDO2 passkeys, biometrics, or cryptographic device-bound credentials. It improves both security and user experience by removing the weakest authentication factor from the equation.
problems_it_solves: Persistent vulnerability from password reuse and phishing
User friction from complex password requirements
Helpdesk cost from password resets
Compliance pressure to eliminate password-based authentication

what_it_is: Email security solutions protect against phishing, business email compromise (BEC), malware attachments, and spam â€” the delivery vector for the majority of enterprise breaches. Modern platforms go beyond signature-based filtering to use AI-based behavioral detection of impersonation and anomalous sender patterns.
problems_it_solves: Spear phishing targeting specific employees
Business email compromise leading to wire fraud
Malware and ransomware delivered via email attachments
Credential harvesting through lookalike login pages

what_it_is: PKI provides the framework of certificates and cryptographic keys that authenticates identities and encrypts communications across the enterprise â€” securing machine-to-machine communication, VPNs, code signing, and website TLS. PKI management platforms automate certificate lifecycle management to prevent outages from expired certificates.
problems_it_solves: Service outages from expired TLS certificates
Unmanaged certificate sprawl across the enterprise
Insecure machine-to-machine communication
Compliance requirements for strong cryptographic identity

what_it_is: OT security solutions protect industrial control systems (ICS), SCADA systems, PLCs, and other operational technology used in manufacturing, energy, utilities, and critical infrastructure. Unlike IT environments, OT systems often run legacy protocols, cannot be patched, and require 24/7 uptime â€” demanding passive, non-disruptive monitoring approaches.
problems_it_solves: Ransomware targeting industrial operations and causing shutdowns
IT/OT network convergence introducing IT threats into OT
Lack of visibility into industrial device communication
Nation-state attacks on critical infrastructure

what_it_is: Mobile security solutions protect corporate data accessed from smartphones and tablets through a combination of mobile threat defense (MTD), mobile device management (MDM), and app-level controls. They detect device compromise, enforce policies, and protect against phishing on mobile platforms.
problems_it_solves: Malicious apps accessing corporate data on mobile devices
Jailbroken or rooted devices bypassing security controls
Phishing attacks delivered via SMS and mobile browsers
Corporate data leakage from personal devices (BYOD)

what_it_is: ASM solutions continuously discover and assess an organization's external attack surface â€” internet-facing assets, exposed services, shadow IT, and digital supply chain â€” from an attacker's perspective. They identify and prioritize unknown or forgotten assets before attackers can exploit them.
problems_it_solves: Unknown internet-exposed assets creating blind spots
Exposed services from shadow IT or forgotten infrastructure
Supply chain and third-party attack surface risk
Slow discovery of newly exposed assets from cloud changes

what_it_is: EASM specifically focuses on the externally visible attack surface â€” domains, subdomains, IP ranges, exposed ports, and third-party dependencies â€” as seen from outside the organization. It provides the attacker's view of the perimeter to help security teams prioritize remediation of the most exposed assets.
problems_it_solves: Subdomain takeover and dangling DNS records
Exposed sensitive ports and services on internet-facing systems
Third-party vendor risk introduced via the supply chain
Continuous perimeter drift as cloud infrastructure changes

what_it_is: Brand protection solutions monitor the internet â€” domains, social media, mobile app stores, dark web markets â€” for impersonation, fake accounts, lookalike sites, and unauthorized use of corporate assets. They detect and facilitate takedowns of malicious infrastructure targeting customers and employees using the organization's brand.
problems_it_solves: Phishing sites impersonating the corporate brand to steal customer credentials
Fake social media accounts conducting fraud
Counterfeit mobile apps targeting customers
Unauthorized use of trademarks and logos online

what_it_is: Data privacy management platforms help organizations comply with privacy regulations (GDPR, CCPA, LGPD) by automating data subject request fulfillment, managing consent records, mapping data flows, and assessing privacy risks. They bridge the gap between legal compliance obligations and technical data management.
problems_it_solves: GDPR and CCPA compliance with data subject rights requests
Lack of visibility into personal data flows across systems
Consent management for marketing and analytics data
Privacy impact assessment for new products and processes

what_it_is: Quantum-safe (or post-quantum) encryption solutions replace current public-key algorithms (RSA, ECC) with cryptographic schemes designed to resist attacks from quantum computers. Organizations handling long-lived sensitive data need to begin migrating now to protect against 'harvest now, decrypt later' attacks.
problems_it_solves: Long-term data confidentiality against future quantum decryption
Harvest now, decrypt later attacks by nation-state adversaries
Cryptographic algorithm agility for post-quantum migration
Compliance with emerging NIST post-quantum standards

what_it_is: Next-generation firewalls inspect network traffic at the application layer, going far beyond traditional port/protocol filtering. They combine stateful inspection, deep packet inspection, intrusion prevention (IPS), application awareness, user identity integration, and TLS decryption into a single platform. Modern NGFWs are deployed at network perimeters, in data centers, and as cloud-native instances to enforce zero-trust segmentation policies.
problems_it_solves: Uncontrolled application usage and shadow IT traversing the network
Encrypted threats hiding in TLS/HTTPS traffic
Lateral movement between network segments
Compliance requirements for network traffic logging and access control
Advanced persistent threats bypassing legacy stateful firewalls

what_it_is: SASE converges wide-area networking (SD-WAN) and cloud-delivered security services â€” including SWG, CASB, ZTNA, and FWaaS â€” into a single cloud-native architecture. Rather than backhauling remote user and branch traffic to a data center firewall, SASE enforces security policy at the cloud edge closest to the user, improving both security and performance for distributed workforces.
problems_it_solves: Slow, unreliable VPN performance for remote and hybrid workforces
Security policy gaps as users access cloud apps directly without inspection
Complex multi-vendor security stacks with inconsistent policy enforcement
Lack of zero-trust network access for third-party and contractor users
Branch office connectivity and security managed through separate tools

what_it_is: ITDR solutions detect and respond to attacks targeting identity infrastructure â€” Active Directory, Entra ID, Okta, and other identity providers. They monitor for credential attacks (pass-the-hash, Kerberoasting, Golden Ticket), privilege escalation, and account compromise in real time, filling the gap that EDR and SIEM leave around identity-layer threats.
problems_it_solves: Active Directory attacks such as DCSync, Kerberoasting, and Golden Ticket forgery
Credential stuffing and password spraying against identity providers
Privilege escalation via identity misconfigurations
Delayed detection of identity-based lateral movement
Blind spots in SIEM/EDR coverage of Active Directory event logs

what_it_is: GRC platforms unify an organisation's approach to corporate governance, enterprise risk management, and regulatory compliance into a single framework. They automate policy management, risk assessments, audit workflows, and compliance reporting, replacing disconnected spreadsheets and manual processes.
problems_it_solves: Fragmented risk registers and policy documentation across departments
Manual audit evidence collection consuming security team time
Inability to demonstrate compliance posture to regulators and boards in real time
Lack of visibility into third-party and vendor risk exposure
Slow response to new regulatory requirements (NCA-ECC, SAMA, GDPR, ISO 27001)

what_it_is: Human risk management platforms reduce security incidents caused by employee behaviour through adaptive security awareness training, phishing simulations, and behavioural risk scoring. They move beyond annual checkbox training to continuous, personalised learning that targets individuals with elevated risk profiles.
problems_it_solves: Employees clicking phishing links despite traditional security training
No visibility into which individuals or teams represent the highest behavioural risk
Compliance with mandatory security awareness requirements (NCA-ECC, ISO 27001, SAMA)
Difficulty measuring training effectiveness and demonstrating risk reduction
Insider threat risk from careless or negligent employee actions
